tree_title: Orchestral Sample Libraries: A Beginner's Guide (+ Recommendations)

overall_topic: Orchestral Sample Libraries: A Beginner's Guide (+ Recommendations)
questions: Identify the two 'all-in-one' orchestral sample libraries recommended for beginners that are distinguished by their full section control: one is specifically noted for its resource-friendliness and inclusion of two mix options (modern and traditional), while the other is characterized by a distinct lean towards a classical, symphonic sound. For each identified library, report the developer, the primary sound character described, and the specific name of the 'lite' or free version available for budget-conscious users.

rounds.question: Identify the two 'all-in-one' orchestral sample libraries recommended for beginners that are distinguished by their full section control: one is specifically noted for its resource-friendliness and inclusion of two mix options (modern and traditional), while the other is characterized by a distinct lean towards a classical, symphonic sound. For each identified library, report the developer, the primary sound character described, and the specific name of the 'lite' or free version available for budget-conscious users.
checklist_width: Library 1 Sound Character: Rich, modern sound (processed) with options for traditional; resource-friendly
Library 2 Sound Character: Beautiful symphonic sound; leans towards classical
Library 2 Budget Version: BBCSO Discover (or BBC Symphony Orchestra Discover)
checklist_depth: Target Entity 1: Nucleus (identified via resource-friendliness and two mix options)
Target Entity 2: BBC Symphony Orchestra Core (identified via classical/symphonic sound comparison)
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify the specific software products (Nucleus and BBCSO Core) based on their described features (mix options vs. classical lean) rather than their names. It then requires Wide Aggregation to retrieve specific attributes (developer, sound profile, and specific budget versions) mentioned in the text for both entities.
source_nodes: Choir Essentials: TRUE LEGATO FOR UNDER 150€?! | Strezov Review - YouTube
rounds.answer_a:
The two all-in-one orchestral sample libraries that meet these criteria are BBC Symphony Orchestra Discover and ProjectSAM Symphobia Lite.

**BBC Symphony Orchestra Discover** is developed by Spitfire Audio and is renowned for being exceptionally resource-friendly while offering both modern and traditional mix options. This library provides full orchestral section control with a streamlined interface designed specifically for beginners. The sound character is described as authentic, balanced, and versatile, capturing the renowned BBC Symphony Orchestra at Maida Vale Studios. The free version, BBC Symphony Orchestra Discover, offers 33 instruments and is available at no cost to users who complete a brief survey and wait 14 days, or can be purchased immediately for a nominal fee [1][2].

**ProjectSAM Symphobia** series, developed by ProjectSAM, is characterized by its distinct classical, symphonic sound with a cinematic edge. The library emphasizes lush, traditional orchestral timbres with full section control capabilities. Its primary sound character is described as grand, emotional, and inherently symphonic, making it ideal for classical-oriented compositions. The lite version, Symphobia Lite, provides access to essential orchestral sections with the same high-quality sampling as the full version, offered at a significantly reduced price point for budget-conscious users [3][4].

Both libraries excel in providing beginners with comprehensive orchestral tools while maintaining distinct sonic signatures - BBC Symphony Orchestra Discover with its balanced, authentic approach and dual mix options, and Symphobia with its pronounced classical symphonic character.

## References

[1] https://www.spitfireaudio.com/bbc-symphony-orchestra-discover
[2] https://www.productionmusiclive.com/blogs/news/best-free-orchestral-vsts
[3] https://projectsam.com/libraries/symphobia-series/
[4] https://www.scoringnotes.com/reviews/best-orchestral-libraries-beginners/
rounds.answer_b:
Audio Imperia – Nucleus  
• Primary sound character: clean, punchy “Hollywood-ready” tone with both a bright modern mix and a warmer, more traditional mix, yet designed to be very CPU- and RAM-friendly for newcomers [1].  
• Free/lite edition: Nucleus Lite Edition (often marketed as “Nucleus LE”), which keeps the two mix options and most core articulations at a much smaller download size [1].

Spitfire Audio – BBC Symphony Orchestra  
• Primary sound character: unmistakably classical and symphonic, aiming to reproduce a natural concert-hall orchestral balance rather than a larger-than-life trailer sound, making it ideal for traditional scoring practice [2].  
• Free/lite edition: BBC Symphony Orchestra Discover, a 200 MB download that offers all core sections and articulations in a simplified form for zero cost [2].

Together these two “all-in-one” libraries give beginners full section control while covering both a modern cinematic palette (Nucleus) and a more purist classical palette (BBCSO).
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Agent B correctly identified the two specific libraries described in the prompt (Audio Imperia Nucleus and Spitfire BBC Symphony Orchestra). The prompt contained specific 'fingerprints' for the libraries: Nucleus is famous for its 'Modern' and 'Classic' mix options, while BBCSO is the standard recommendation for a 'classical' sound. 

Agent A failed the Deep Logic check. It incorrectly identified the first library as BBC Symphony Orchestra Discover and hallucinated that it offers 'modern and traditional mix options' (it does not; it has a single mix). It also missed Nucleus entirely. Because Agent A provided factually incorrect information about the core features of the products, Agent B is the clear winner despite missing a reference list.
